step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{D}
The provided medical evidence repeatedly emphasizes the role of vitamin D in promoting bone health and inhibiting bone resorption, particularly when combined with calcium sources like calcium oyster shell. While calcium-containing materials such as hooves or pinna are mentioned indirectly, only mollusk shells are explicitly described in relation to biomineralization — a process involving the incorporation of metals/inorganics into biological structures. Thus, molluscan shells are a classic example of biomineralized tissue formed via deposition of calcium carbonate and other minerals. Therefore, option D best aligns with the definition given in the query regarding biomineralization.
